perturbation.original_text: SIERRA warrants that it carries general liability insurance of not less than $2 million per occurrence and product liability insurance of not less than $5 million per occurrence and that, upon the execution of this Agreement, it will name ENVISION as an additional insured on such policies.
perturbation.changed_text: SIERRA warrants that it will endeavor to carry general liability insurance and product liability insurance, and will attempt to name ENVISION as an additional insured on such policies.
perturbation.explanation: The modification weakens the obligation to carry specific insurance coverage and name ENVISION as an additional insured. The phrases 'endeavor to carry' and 'attempt to name' introduce ambiguity, making compliance uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Colorado Revised Statutes § 13-21-111.5 - Negligence or fault -- product liability actions -- limitations
perturbation.law_explanation: CRS § 13-21-111.5 concerns product liability actions and implies a reasonable standard of care in manufacturing and distribution to avoid liability. By weakening the warranty to 'endeavor' to carry and 'attempt' to include ENVISION on the policy, the contract creates a loophole that could allow SIERRA to avoid adequate insurance coverage, potentially hindering ENVISION's ability to seek recourse in case of a product liability claim. This creates uncertainty around the allocation of risk and ENVISION's ability to be compensated under CRS § 13-21-111.5 for product liability.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces ambiguity by replacing firm obligations ('warrants') with weaker, aspirational language ('endeavor to carry', 'attempt to name'). This directly undermines the original contract's intent to ensure specific insurance coverage and additional insured status for ENVISION. The 'Ambiguity Legal Contradiction' definition states that a legal contradiction occurs when an obligation is introduced ambiguously, making it difficult to enforce under state or national law, which aligns with the changes made in the contract. The changed text leaves compliance uncertain and could allow SIERRA to avoid fulfilling the insurance requirements, which creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ENVISION will use its best efforts to market and sell the Finished Product throughout the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: ENVISION will use reasonable efforts to market and sell the Finished Product throughout the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "best efforts" to "reasonable efforts" weakens the obligation. "Best efforts" is a higher standard, requiring diligent and persistent action, while "reasonable efforts" allows for more subjective interpretation and less strenuous activity.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-306 implies a good faith obligation in exclusive dealing arrangements. While 'reasonable efforts' might seem compliant on the surface, it falls short if it leads to a disproportionately low sales volume compared to what could be achieved with 'best efforts.' This is particularly relevant as Colorado has adopted the UCC. An exclusive distributor is expected to put forth a high level of effort, and diminishing that to 'reasonable efforts' contradicts the implicit obligation of good faith and diligent performance associated with an exclusive distributorship under UCC 2-306. ENVISION could fulfill the 'reasonable efforts' clause with minimal investment, not maximizing the potential of the product or market penetration, contradicting the spirit of an exclusive agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-306. Output, Requirements and Exclusive Dealings. (1) A term which measures the quantity by the output of the seller or the requirements of the buyer means such actual output or requirements as may occur in good faith , except that no quantity unreasonably disproportionate to any stated estimate or in the absence of a stated estimate to any normal or otherwise comparable prior output or requirements may be tendered or demanded. (2) A lawful agreement by either the seller or the buyer for exclusive dealing in the kind of goods concerned imposes unless otherwise agreed an obligation by the seller to use best efforts to supply the goods and by the buyer to use best efforts to promote their sale . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The change from "best efforts" to "reasonable efforts" creates a contradiction, weakening the obligation under UCC § 2-306 regarding exclusive dealings. Scraped Law Snippet 1 explicitly states that a lawful agreement for exclusive dealing "imposes unless otherwise agreed an obligation by the seller to use best efforts to supply the goods and by the buyer to use best efforts to promote their sale." By changing "best efforts" to "reasonable efforts," the modified text contradicts this implied obligation, as 'reasonable efforts' represents a lower standard of performance than 'best efforts,' potentially undermining the expected level of diligence in an exclusive agreement.
